shows.syn: The Lost Boys: A New Musical is an upcoming 2026 musical with music and lyrics by American indie pop/rock band the Rescues and a book by David Hornsby and Chris Hoch. It is based on the 1987 comedy horror cult classic film The Lost Boys.

ct.n: Lauren Yalango-Grant and Christopher Cree Grant

shows.syn: August Wilson's drama set in a Pittsburgh boarding house in 1911, where a mysterious man searches for his wife after years of forced servitude. Part of Wilson's acclaimed Pittsburgh Cycle chronicling the African-American experience decade by decade. This 2026 revival is directed by Debbie Allen at the Ethel Barrymore Theatre.
Filled with fun, frolics and frocks, this is the show the Daily Telegraph calls “fresh, subversive, and essential.”
Beaches is a stage musical with a book by Iris Rainer Dart and Thom Thomas, with music by Mike Stoller and lyrics by Dart. The show is based on the 1985 novel of the same name by Dart.
The Balusters is a comedy play written by David Lindsay-Abaire and originally produced at Manhattan Theatre Club.
Schmigadoon! is a stage musical adaptation of the first season of the musical television series Schmigadoon!, with book, music, and lyrics by Cinco Paul.
Fallen Angels is a comedy by the English playwright Noël Coward. It opened at the Globe Theatre (now called the Gielgud Theatre), London, on 21 April 1925 and ran until 29 August.

A young woman struggles with the legacy of her brilliant but mentally ill mathematician father, questioning whether she has inherited his genius, his madness, or both — and whether a groundbreaking proof found in his notebooks is truly his work, or hers. David Auburn's Pulitzer Prize-winning play returns to Broadway.

Based on the acclaimed 2015 documentary, this play tells the extraordinary true story of Nick Yarris, an American wrongfully convicted and sentenced to death row, who spent over two decades fighting to prove his innocence. Starring Adrien Brody and Tessa Thompson.

Watch Dion (Mindelle) crash a tour group at a Titanic museum to tell them what really happened to Jack and Rose — and it's not how Hollywood tells it. The whole thing is set to Dion's hits (including, of course, "My Heart Will Go On") and a nonstop wave of movie spoofs and up-to-the-minute pop culture jokes.

ct.n: Tye Blue, Marla Mindelle, and Constantine Rousouli

shows.syn: Arthur Miller's Pulitzer Prize-winning masterpiece about Willy Loman, a traveling salesman grappling with the collapse of the American Dream as his career and family life unravel. This 2026 revival at the Winter Garden Theatre is directed by Joe Mantello.

A radical reimagining of Andrew Lloyd Webber's iconic dance musical, inspired by the Ballroom culture that emerged from New York City. Staged as an immersive competition, this production features all-new Ballroom and club beats, runway-ready choreography, and a dazzling makeover that celebrates the joyous transformation of self at the heart of both Cats and Ballroom.
Becky Shaw is a play written by Gina Gionfriddo. The play premiered at the Humana Festival in 2008 and opened Off-Broadway in 2008. The play was a finalist for the 2009 Pulitzer Prize for Drama.
A new play by Pulitzer Prize winner Stephen Adly Guirgis, based on the legendary true crime story of the 1972 Brooklyn bank robbery that became a hostage crisis and media circus.

Set in summer 1983, Roald Dahl faces the fallout from a recently published antisemitic article just as The Witches is about to be released. Over one fraught afternoon at his family home, he must choose between making a public apology or defending his reputation. Winner of three Olivier Awards.

"Every Brilliant Thing" is a heartwarming and poignant one-person play that takes the audience on an emotional journey. As the protagonist recounts their personal story, they invite the audience to participate, creating a unique and interactive experience that explores the beauty and fragility of life. Through humor and vulnerability, the play celebrates the small moments that make life worth living, leaving a lasting impact on all who witness it.

Bug is a play by American playwright Tracy Letts. Exploring themes of paranoia and conspiracy theories, the play tells the story of a woman who, as she spends time with a newly acquainted man in her motel room, starts sharing more and more of his paranoias.

All Out: Comedy About Ambition is a show from Simon Rich which consists of live readings of  short stories about "ego, envy, greed, and basically just New Yorkers in general." The first production, directed by Alex Timbers, opens on December 12, 2025, at the Nederlander Theatre in New York City on Broadway and is scheduled to close on March 8, 2026.

In the near future, a woman in her eighties named Marjorie spends time with a holographic projection of her late husband. As her memory fades, her family grapples with what it means to remember and be remembered.

shows.t: Two Strangers (Carry a Cake Across New York)
shows.syn: A new musical comedy about two strangers who meet on the streets of New York City when Robin agrees to help Dougal carry a cake across town. As they navigate the city together, unexpected connection and romance bloom in this charming, laugh-filled love story celebrating chance encounters and the magic of New York.
The Cold War chess musical with music by ABBA's Benny Andersson and Björn Ulvaeus finally arrives on Broadway.

Robert Icke transforms Sophocles' epic tragedy into an essential, explosive human thriller. Starring Mark Strong and Lesley Manville, this Olivier Award-winning production catapults the secrets of the past into a high-stakes present on election night.
Based on the documentary film, this musical tells the story of Jackie Siegel and her family's quest to build the largest house in America.

ct.n: Lauren Yalango-Grant and Christopher Cree Grant

shows.syn: In the outer limits of rural Idaho, the last two estranged members of the Fernsby family — a reclusive aunt and her gay nephew — reunite to sort the mess left behind after a troubled father's passing. An intimate exploration of generational disconnect and reconciliation.

Liberation is a 2025 American memory play by Bess Wohl. Developed by the Roundabout Theatre Company, it explores second-wave feminism, memory, and the complexities of social change across generations.
The sweeping musical about three families in turn-of-the-century America returns to Broadway.

Lydia Deetz is a strange and unusual teenager obsessed with the whole "being dead thing." Lucky for Lydia, her new house is haunted by a recently deceased couple and a degenerate demon who happens to have a thing for stripes. When Lydia calls on this ghost-with-the-most to scare away her insufferable parents, Beetlejuice comes up with the perfect plan, which involves exorcism, arranged marriages and an adorable girl scout who gets scared out of her wits.

Based on the true story of Jacob Dunne, who at 19 delivered a single punch that killed a stranger on a night out. After prison, he meets his victim's parents, setting in motion an extraordinary journey of restorative justice and redemption.
Samuel Beckett's tragicomedy about two men waiting by a lonely tree for the arrival of the mysterious Godot.

Three longtime friends are thrown into turmoil when one of them buys an expensive modern painting — a plain white canvas with faint white lines. The purchase ignites a heated debate about art, taste, money, and the nature of friendship itself.
ABBA's hits tell the story of a young woman's search for her birth father on a Greek island paradise. A sunny, funny tale of love, laughter and friendship.

In 1989 rural Louisiana, Isabelle "Izzy" Scutley is trapped in an abusive marriage but sustains herself through her secret gift for poetry. A darkly comic solo play about one woman's fight to reclaim her voice.

Based on the beloved play and film, this new musical follows Ana, a first-generation Mexican-American teenager in East LA, torn between her dreams of going to college and her family's expectations. With an original Latin pop-infused score, it's a joyful celebration of family, identity, and self-acceptance.

A darkly hilarious musical about the bizarre true story of Elmer McCurdy, a bumbling outlaw who died in a 1911 shootout in Oklahoma. His unclaimed, embalmed body spent the next 65 years on a macabre odyssey as a sideshow attraction and carnival prop, until a TV crew discovered the mummy hanging in a Long Beach amusement park ride in 1976. With music and lyrics by David Yazbek and Erik Della Penna and a book by Itamar Moses.

ct.n: Erik Della Penna, Dean Sharenow and David Yazbek

shows.syn: A new musical celebrating the life and music of Bobby Darin.

In the rollicking musical "Pirates! The Penzance Musical," a young man named Frederic is mistakenly apprenticed to a band of bumbling pirates, leading to a hilarious clash of duty and desire as he falls for the beautiful Mabel and must choose between his love and his loyalty. With swashbuckling adventure, soaring music, and a heart-warming story, this lighthearted production at the Todd Haimes Theatre promises a delightful evening of pure theatrical enchantment.
Hawkins, 1959: A regular American town where nothing strange happens. Until a young student arrives, fleeing the horrors of childhood, and everything changes. The prequel to the hit Netflix series.

In the depths of a Kentucky cave, a man named Floyd Collins is trapped, sparking a media frenzy and a desperate race against time to save him. As the world watches, Floyd's family and the local community grapple with the weight of the tragedy, their emotions and relationships tested by the unfolding drama. "Floyd Collins" is a haunting and poignant musical that explores the human spirit's resilience in the face of unimaginable adversity.

John Proctor Is the Villain is a stage play written by American playwright Kimberly Belflower. It is a revisionist take on the classic American play The Crucible by Arthur Miller, centering on a group of modern-day high school students and their interpretation of the historical events the play is based on.

Smash is an American musical with music by Marc Shaiman and lyrics by Shaiman and Scott Wittman, and a book by Rick Elice and Bob Martin. Based on the 2012–2013 NBC television series of the same name created by Theresa Rebeck, the musical includes songs from the TV series and an original finale song written for the stage.

Stephen Sondheim's Old Friends is a tribute revue honoring musical theatre composer and lyricist Stephen Sondheim devised and produced by Cameron Mackintosh.
Originally designed as a one-night performance, the revue premiered at the Sondheim Theatre in London on 3 May 2022.
The Last Five Years is a musical written by Jason Robert Brown. It premiered at Chicago's Northlight Theatre in 2001 and was then produced Off-Broadway in March 2002.
Betty Boop makes her Broadway debut in this new musical celebrating the iconic cartoon character.

"Good Night, and Good Luck" takes audiences on a captivating journey through the high-stakes world of 1950s television. As journalist Edward R. Murrow risks his career to challenge the McCarthy era's rampant fear-mongering, the play explores the delicate balance between press freedom and political power. With its gripping narrative and powerful performances, this production transports viewers to a pivotal moment in history, where one man's courageous stand against injustice resonates with profound relevance in today's landscape.

In the cutthroat world of real estate, a group of ruthless salesmen compete for the chance to clinch the ultimate deal. As tensions rise and desperation sets in, their moral compass is tested, and they must confront the ruthless realities of their industry. "Glengarry Glen Ross" is a riveting exploration of the American dream gone awry, where the pursuit of success comes at a heavy personal cost.

A young, beautiful man named Dorian Gray strikes a Faustian bargain - his portrait will age while he remains forever youthful. As Dorian indulges in a life of hedonism and sin, his portrait becomes a mirror for his corrupted soul, leading to a chilling confrontation with his own mortality. This dark, Gothic tale of vanity and the corrupting nature of absolute beauty captivates audiences in this thrilling stage adaptation of Oscar Wilde's classic novel.

The Tragedy of Othello, the Moor of Venice, often shortened to Othello, is a tragedy written by William Shakespeare around 1603. Set in Venice and Cyprus, the play depicts the Moorish military commander Othello as he is manipulated by his ensign, Iago, into suspecting his wife Desdemona of infidelity.
The Olivier Award-winning Best New Musical tells the outrageous true story of the WWII plot to fool Hitler with a corpse, fake documents, and an idealistic intelligence team.
A musical celebration of the legendary Cuban musicians who came together to record the Grammy-winning album.

Purpose is a stage play written by American playwright Branden Jacobs-Jenkins. It premiered at the Steppenwolf Theatre in Chicago in 2023–24. The play is a family drama revealing the members of the black, politically prominent Jasper family.

After her son's death, Jesse leaves her wife and work, drives across the US to the redwood forest, and forms a profound relationship with a giant redwood tree. Inspired by the life of Julia Butterfly Hill, this new musical explores grief, connection, and the healing power of nature.
English is a play by Sanaz Toossi. The play explores themes of language, identity, and cultural assimilation, set in an English-language classroom in Karaj, Iran, in 2008.

All In: Comedy About Love is a show from Simon Rich which consists of live readings of previously published short stories from The New Yorker. The first production, directed by Alex Timbers, began previews on December 11, 2024, at the Hudson Theater in New York City on Broadway; before opening on December 22, 2024.

"Gypsy" follows the ambitious stage mother Rose as she pushes her two daughters, June and Louise, to stardom in the world of vaudeville. As the sisters struggle to find their own paths, Rose's relentless drive threatens to tear their family apart. With show-stopping musical numbers and a gripping family drama, "Gypsy" explores the cost of achieving the American dream and the unbreakable bond between a mother and her children.

In the heart of a progressive community, the parents of Eureka Day School must navigate the complexities of a measles outbreak. As they grapple with conflicting beliefs and values, tensions rise, and the search for consensus becomes a fierce battleground. With sharp wit and keen insight, this timely play explores the challenges of maintaining unity in the face of growing division.

In Leslye Headland's cuttingly hilarious family dramedy, four adult siblings and their partners return to their childhood home in Connecticut for Christmas Eve. As old conflicts resurface around sexuality, religion, addiction, and obligation, the Dahl family's harmonious traditions give way to discord. The final installment in Headland's Seven Deadly Plays cycle, exploring how the family unit can become a cult of its own.
Based on the cult classic film, two rivals discover a magical potion that promises eternal youth, with hilarious and deadly consequences.

A musical featuring the songs of The Avett Brothers with a book by John Logan. In 1888, a violent storm sinks a New England whaling ship, leaving four survivors stranded on a lifeboat with no food or water. As days turn to weeks, the men face an impossible moral reckoning inspired by a harrowing true story. Directed by Michael Mayer, the show runs 90 minutes with no intermission.

Buddy, a human raised by elves, embarks on a whimsical journey to New York City to find his birth father and discover his true identity. As he navigates the bustling streets and encounters the cynical world of adults, Buddy's infectious enthusiasm and childlike wonder bring joy and laughter, transforming the lives of those around him and reminding everyone of the true spirit of Christmas.

In the captivating musical "Tammy Faye," audiences are swept into the extraordinary life of the beloved televangelist. Navigating the highs and lows of fame, Tammy Faye's unwavering faith and infectious charisma captivate a nation, even as she confronts the scandals that threaten to tear her world apart. With a powerful score and a remarkable performance, this poignant tale explores the complexities of love, redemption, and the enduring power of the human spirit.
In a future where robots have become obsolete, two helper-bots discover what it means to be human through an unexpected connection. Winner of six 2025 Tony Awards including Best Musical.

shows.t: A Wonderful World: The Louis Armstrong Musical
shows.v: Harold and Miriam Steinberg Center for Theatre
shows.syn: "A Wonderful World: The Louis Armstrong Musical" transports audiences into the life and times of the iconic jazz legend. Through soulful melodies and captivating performances, the show chronicles Armstrong's journey from a young street performer to a global superstar, exploring the triumphs and challenges that shape his remarkable career and enduring legacy as a musical pioneer and cultural icon.

Directed by Sam Gold and staged in the round at Circle in the Square, this boldly reimagined production of Shakespeare's tragedy stars Kit Connor as Romeo and Rachel Zegler as Juliet. Featuring original music by Jack Antonoff and movement by Sonya Tayeh, the production brings a raw, contemporary energy aimed at a new generation of theatergoers.
Left on Tenth is a stage play written by American writer Delia Ephron, based on her autobiography of the same name.

In the glitz and glamour of 1950s Hollywood, a struggling screenwriter stumbles upon the forgotten mansion of a reclusive silent film star, Norma Desmond. Captivated by her faded glory, he becomes ensnared in her obsession to make a comeback, leading them on a twisted path of manipulation, desperation, and the ultimate demise of their doomed romance. "Sunset Boulevard" is a haunting, atmospheric musical that delves into the dark underbelly of the entertainment industry, where dreams and illusions collide with devastating consequences.

ct.n: David Cullen and Andrew Lloyd Webber

shows.syn: Thornton Wilders beloved American classic starring Jim Parsons and Katie Holmes.

Yellow Face is a semi-autobiographical play by David Henry Hwang, featuring the author himself as the protagonist, DHH, mounting his 1993 play Face Value. The play's themes include questions of race and of the interaction between media and politics.
McNeal is a one-act stage play written by Ayad Akhtar.
The Hills of California is a play written by British playwright Jez Butterworth.
A play by Jen Silverman starring Patti LuPone and Mia Farrow as unlikely roommates.

"Once Upon a Mattress" is a delightful musical romp that reimagines the classic fairy tale of "The Princess and the Pea." Feisty Princess Winnifred shakes up the kingdom as she competes for the hand of the shy Prince Dauntless, much to the dismay of his overbearing mother, Queen Aggravain. With witty dialogue, catchy tunes, and a touch of slapstick humor, this enchanting production keeps audiences enthralled as it weaves a charming tale of love, laughter, and the power of being true to oneself.

Job is a play written by Max Wolf Friedlich. The original production started on September 6, 2023, at the Soho Playhouse in New York City. The play revolves around a young woman Jane (Sydney Lemmon) with her therapist Loyd (Peter Friedman).

Cole Escola's uproarious comedy imagines Mary Todd Lincoln as a miserable, hard-drinking woman who dreams of becoming a cabaret star while her husband prepares for that fateful night at Ford's Theatre.

PlayStation Home was a virtual 3D social gaming platform developed by Sony Computer Entertainment's London Studio for the PlayStation 3 (PS3) on the PlayStation Network (PSN). It was accessible from the PS3's XrossMediaBar (XMB).
F. Scott Fitzgerald's iconic novel roars to life in this dazzling new musical. Jay Gatsby throws lavish parties in pursuit of his lost love, Daisy Buchanan, in the glittering Jazz Age.
Paula Vogel's autobiographical memory play exploring her complicated relationship with her mother through decades of family history.
A dance musical set to Sufjan Stevens' iconic album 'Illinois', telling stories of love, loss, and hope through breathtaking choreography.
Chekhov's masterpiece about a family gathering at a rural estate where simmering resentments and unrequited loves come to the surface.
A profound drama about a single mother caring for her medically fragile son, finding community among the nurses, therapists, and caregivers who help her.

shows.t: The Heart of Rock and Roll
shows.syn: A jukebox musical featuring the songs of Huey Lewis and the News, following Bobby who must choose between his rock star dreams and a stable job.
A political thriller about Boris Berezovsky's role in Vladimir Putin's rise to power, and the oligarch's subsequent fall from grace.

shows.t: Cabaret at the Kit Kat Club
shows.syn: Willkommen to the Kit Kat Club! This immersive, Tony-winning revival transforms the theater into a 1930s Berlin nightclub. Starring Eddie Redmayne as the Emcee.

ct.n: Meghann Zervoulis Bate and Anessa Marie Scolpini

shows.syn: A new musical featuring the songs of 16-time Grammy Award winner Alicia Keys. Set in 1990s New York, a 17-year-old girl dreams of a life beyond her Hell's Kitchen block.
Set in a 1970s recording studio, this Pulitzer Prize-winning play follows a rock band creating their masterpiece as personal tensions threaten to tear them apart.
The story of the American women's suffrage movement, following Alice Paul and her fellow suffragettes as they fight for the 19th Amendment.
The Tony-winning African American retelling of The Wizard of Oz with a soulful R&B score. Starring Wayne Brady and Deborah Cox.
The story of bold Art Deco painter Tamara de Lempicka, exploring her artistic ambition, passionate love affairs, and escape from war-torn Europe.

Based on S.E. Hinton's classic novel, this electrifying new musical follows the Greasers and Socs in 1967 Tulsa as they navigate loyalty, love, and the struggle to belong. Winner of the 2024 Tony Award for Best Musical.
A deaf, dumb, and blind boy becomes a pinball champion in this rock opera. The acclaimed revival directed by Des McAnuff featuring The Who's iconic score.
Based on the bestselling novel, this Depression-era love story unfolds at a traveling circus where a young veterinarian falls for the star performer.
Ibsen's classic play about a doctor who discovers the town's water supply is contaminated and faces fierce opposition when trying to expose the truth.
Based on Nicholas Sparks' beloved novel, this musical tells the epic love story of Noah and Allie across decades, featuring three couples at different stages of their relationship.

ct.n: David Zinn and Brett J. Banakis

shows.syn: A nun becomes suspicious of a priest's relationship with a young student at a Bronx Catholic school in this Pulitzer Prize-winning drama.
A new musical adaptation of the classic 1962 film about two people who fall in love and descend into alcoholism together. With music by Adam Guettel and book by Craig Lucas.
An epic family drama spanning generations, exploring the lives of a Jewish family in France grappling with rising antisemitism and questions of identity.
Three estranged siblings reunite to settle their late father's estate in Arkansas, where a disturbing discovery forces them to confront dark family secrets.

Based on the documentary, this groundbreaking musical follows seven autistic young adults preparing for their first spring formal dance. Historic for featuring autistic actors in all seven lead roles.
The Tony Award-winning musical comedy lovingly ripped off from Monty Python and the Holy Grail, following King Arthur and his knights on their quest for the Holy Grail.
The story of the Comedian Harmonists, a singing group in 1920s and 30s Germany who became international stars before being silenced by the Nazi regime.
A darkly comic new play about a man buried alive in his stuff and the estranged family members trying to help him.
A two-man comedy musical about aspiring playwrights who present their ridiculous musical about Johannes Gutenberg using hats to play all the characters.
Sondheim's musical told in reverse, following three friends—a composer, a playwright, and a writer—from middle-age success back to their hopeful youth. Tony-winning 2023 revival.
A vibrant comedy set in a Harlem hair braiding salon on the hottest day of summer, where the lives of West African immigrant women intertwine.

shows.t: Purlie Victorious: A Non-Confederate Romp Through the Cotton Patch
shows.syn: Purlie Victorious (A Non-Confederate Romp through the Cotton Patch) is a three-act comedic stage play written by American actor Ossie Davis. It tells the fictional story of Reverend Purlie Victorious Judson, a dynamic traveling preacher returning to rural Georgia to save his small hometown church.
A behind-the-scenes comedy about the chaotic filming of Jaws, as three actors stuck on a boat deal with mechanical failures and clashing egos.

shows.t: Back to the Future: The Musical
shows.syn: Based on the iconic 1985 film, Marty McFly travels back in time in the DeLorean.
A farce set in 1920s England where a wife's infidelity is discovered, leading to a cascade of comic revelations and twists.
An immersive dance-floor musical experience about the rise and fall of Imelda Marcos, First Lady of the Philippines. Features music by David Byrne and Fatboy Slim.
Alex Edelman's solo show about accidentally attending a secret gathering of white supremacists, blending comedy with sharp social commentary.

shows.t: Once Upon a One More Time
shows.syn: A jukebox musical featuring the songs of Britney Spears, where fairy tale princesses discover feminism and rewrite their own stories.
A horror play about a couple who crashes their car in the mountains and seeks shelter in a cabin, only to discover terrifying secrets within.

shows.t: The Sign in Sidney Brustein's Window
shows.syn: The Sign in Sidney Brustein's Window is the second and last staged play by playwright Lorraine Hansberry, author of A Raisin in the Sun.  The play focuses on events that occur after Sidney hangs a political sign urging an end to bossism in the window of his Greenwich Village apartment.

New York, New York is a musical with music by John Kander, lyrics by Fred Ebb and Lin-Manuel Miranda, and a book by David Thompson and Sharon Washington. Inspired by and loosely based on the 1977 film of the same name by Martin Scorsese, the musical premiered on Broadway on April 26, 2023.

Summer, 1976 is a Broadway play written by David Auburn. The story follows two women living in Ohio who become unlikely friends over playdates with their respective young daughters during the summer of 1976, the American Bicentennial.

Good Night, Oscar is a comedy-drama play written by American playwright Doug Wright. The story revolves around a fictional event in which pianist and humorist Oscar Levant portrayed by Sean Hayes appears on The Tonight Show with Jack Paar.

ct.n: Carolina Ortiz Herrera and Ben Stanton

shows.syn: "Prima Facie" is a gripping legal drama that delves into the complexities of the justice system. Tessa, a brilliant young barrister, navigates the challenging terrain of a high-profile case, where her unwavering belief in the law is tested by a shocking personal experience. As she grapples with the ethical dilemmas that arise, Tessa's world is turned upside down, forcing her to confront the very foundations of her profession and her own identity.

In a hilarious blend of satire and social commentary, "The Thanksgiving Play" follows a group of well-meaning but misguided educators as they attempt to stage a politically correct Thanksgiving play. Navigating the minefield of cultural sensitivities and personal agendas, the characters hilariously clash, exposing the complexities and contradictions inherent in their efforts to represent the holiday in an inclusive and respectful manner. The play offers a biting, yet compassionate, exploration of the challenges of navigating modern-day societal divides.
Peter Pan Goes Wrong is a comedy play by Henry Lewis, Jonathan Sayer, and Henry Shields of the Mischief Theatre company, creators of The Play That Goes Wrong (2012).
Camelot is a musical with music by Frederick Loewe and lyrics and a book by Alan Jay Lerner. It is based on the legend of King Arthur as adapted from the 1958 novel The Once and Future King by T.

ct.n: Robert Russell Bennett and Philip J. Lang

shows.syn:
Fat Ham is a dramatic stage play written by American playwright James Ijames. It is a modern-day adaptation of William Shakespeare's Hamlet.
The play was awarded the 2022 Pulitzer Prize for Drama and was nominated for five Tony Awards including Best Play.

"Shucked" takes audiences on a whimsical journey through the heart of rural America, where a quirky band of farmers and a city-slicker outsider team up to save their struggling corn farm. With a toe-tapping score and a cast of unforgettable characters, this heartwarming musical comedy celebrates the power of community, the magic of unexpected friendships, and the enduring spirit of the heartland.

Trapped on a lifeboat with a Bengal tiger, a young Indian boy named Pi must use his wits and courage to survive a harrowing journey across the vast Pacific Ocean. As they face treacherous storms and the constant threat of the tiger's power, Pi's resilience and his remarkable bond with the ferocious animal become the keys to his remarkable tale of perseverance and self-discovery.

Sweeney Todd, the infamous "Demon Barber of Fleet Street," returns to London seeking revenge against the judge who wrongfully imprisoned him. With the help of his resourceful neighbor, Mrs. Lovett, he opens a barbershop where he lures unsuspecting customers to their gruesome demise, the bodies of which Mrs. Lovett bakes into her famous meat pies. As Sweeney's thirst for vengeance grows, so too does the body count in this gripping, suspenseful musical thriller.

Andrew Lloyd Webber's Cinderella, produced on Broadway as Bad Cinderella, is a musical with music by Andrew Lloyd Webber, lyrics by David Zippel, and a book by Emerald Fennell. Loosely adapted from the classic Cinderella story, the musical recasts gender relationships, explores the theme of beauty shaming, and Cinderella changes her appearance to secure love, but discovers it is better to be true to oneself.

"Bob Fosse's Dancin'" takes audiences on a mesmerizing journey through the iconic choreographer's world. With a dazzling ensemble of dancers, the show explores the raw passion and unbridled energy of Fosse's groundbreaking style, blending jazz, ballet, and avant-garde movements into a captivating spectacle. From the sultry sways to the sharp, syncopated steps, the production celebrates the timeless allure of Fosse's legendary choreography, transporting viewers into the heart of the Broadway experience.

"Parade" tells the gripping true story of the 1913 trial of Leo Frank, a Jewish factory manager accused of murdering a young girl in Atlanta. As the trial unfolds, the community's antisemitism and political tensions threaten to tear the city apart. With soaring melodies and a powerful narrative, this Tony-winning musical explores the pursuit of justice and the human cost of prejudice in early 20th-century America.

ct.n: Lauren Yalango-Grant and Christopher Cree Grant
Don Sebesky and Jason Robert Brown

shows.syn: Nora Helmer's seemingly perfect life as a 19th-century housewife is upended when she is forced to confront the harsh realities of her marriage and the societal constraints that have long defined her. As Nora's facade begins to crumble, she must make a bold decision that will forever alter the course of her life and challenge the very foundations of her society. "A Doll's House" is a gripping exploration of personal freedom, gender roles, and the power of self-determination.

shows.v: Harold and Miriam Steinberg Center for Theatre
shows.syn: Pictures from Home is a memory play written by Sharr White based on the book Pictures from Home by photographer Larry Sultan. The first production, directed by Bartlett Sher, opened in previews on 13 January 2023 at the Studio 54 theatre in New York City on Broadway.

ct.n: Scott Lehrer and Peter John Still

shows.syn: "Between Riverside and Crazy" is a gripping exploration of a retired police officer's fight for justice and redemption. As he navigates the complexities of his personal relationships and the legal system, he finds himself embroiled in a high-stakes battle that challenges his values and tests the limits of his resilience. With its powerful performances and thought-provoking narrative, this play takes audiences on a riveting journey that delves into the heart of social issues and the human condition.
The Collaboration is a dramatic stage play written by New Zealand playwright Anthony McCarten. The play originated in the West End at The Young Vic in London.

Two struggling musicians, Joe and Jerry, witness a mob murder and must go on the run. Disguising themselves as women, they join an all-female band and end up entangled in a hilarious web of mistaken identities, high-stakes hijinks, and unexpected romance. Packed with big laughs, dazzling dance numbers, and an irresistible score, "Some Like It Hot" is a wildly entertaining musical adaptation of the classic comedy film.
Ohio State Murders is a play written by Adrienne Kennedy. The play was first published on January 14, 1991.

shows.t: A Beautiful Noise, The Neil Diamond Musical
shows.syn: "A Beautiful Noise, The Neil Diamond Musical" transports audiences on a journey through the life and iconic songs of the legendary Neil Diamond. Blending electrifying performances with a captivating narrative, the musical explores Diamond's rise to stardom, personal triumphs, and the timeless melodies that have touched millions. With an all-star cast bringing his beloved hits to life, this Broadway event promises an unforgettable celebration of one of music's most enduring icons.

ct.n: Bob Gaudio, Sonny Paladino and Brian Usifer

shows.syn: "Ain't No Mo'" is a bold and genre-bending theatrical experience that takes audiences on a satirical journey through the African American experience in modern-day America. Blending sketch comedy, social commentary, and powerful storytelling, the play explores the idea of a hypothetical government program that offers African Americans one-way tickets to Africa, sparking a hilarious and thought-provoking exploration of race, identity, and the complexities of the Black experience. With its sharp wit and unapologetic honesty, "Ain't No Mo'" promises to leave a lasting impact on all who witness its transformative performance.

ct.n: Jonathan Deans and Taylor J. Williams

shows.syn: "KPOP" transports audiences into the high-stakes, high-pressure world of K-pop stardom. Centered around a diverse ensemble of up-and-coming K-pop artists, the musical navigates the intense training, fierce competition, and personal sacrifices required to achieve global fame. Blending electrifying musical numbers with intimate character-driven narratives, "KPOP" offers a captivating peek behind the curtain of the K-pop industry, exploring the dreams, passions, and vulnerabilities of those striving to become the next big K-pop sensation.

In Charles Dickens' beloved classic, A Christmas Carol, the miserly Ebenezer Scrooge is visited by the ghosts of Christmases past, present, and yet to come, who guide him on a transformative journey of redemption. As Scrooge confronts the consequences of his selfish ways, he must choose whether to embrace the true spirit of the holiday and change his ways before it's too late. This timeless tale of hope and second chances comes to life on the Broadway stage, captivating audiences with its timeless message and enchanting theatricality.
What if Juliet's story didn't end in tragedy? This joyful jukebox musical featuring Max Martin's iconic pop hits reimagines Shakespeare's heroine as she writes her own destiny.

shows.t: Mike Birbiglia: The Old Man & The Pool
shows.syn: Mike Birbiglia (; born June 20, 1978) is an American comedian, actor, director, producer and Emmy-nominated writer. He has written and performed a series of award-winning solo shows worldwide.

In "Take Me Out", a star baseball player comes out as gay, sparking a fierce debate among his teammates and the media. As tensions rise, the play delves into the complexities of sexuality, identity, and the challenges of navigating the public spotlight. With a powerful ensemble cast, "Take Me Out" explores the personal and societal implications of one man's decision to live openly in the high-stakes world of professional sports.

"Kimberly Akimbo" is a heartwarming musical that follows the story of a teenager with a rare aging condition who navigates the complexities of family, friendship, and self-discovery. With a sharp wit and an unwavering spirit, Kimberly embarks on a journey to reclaim her childhood and find her place in a world that doesn't always understand her. Blending poignant drama with vibrant musical numbers, "Kimberly Akimbo" explores themes of resilience, acceptance, and the universal desire to live life to the fullest.
Almost Famous is a 2019 stage musical with music by Tom Kitt, lyrics by Cameron Crowe and Kitt, and a book by Crowe. It is based on the 2000 film of the same name written and directed by Crowe.
The White Stripes were an American rock duo formed in Detroit, Michigan, in 1997. The group consisted of Jack White (guitar, keyboards, piano, vocals) and Meg White (drums, percussion, vocals).

In "Topdog/Underdog," two estranged brothers, Lincoln and Booth, navigate the complexities of their shared past and the intense rivalry that threatens to tear them apart. As they struggle to reconcile their differences and find their place in a world that seems stacked against them, the play delves into the nuances of family, identity, and the pursuit of the American Dream.

The Piano Lesson is a powerful family drama that explores the complex legacy of slavery and the struggle to reclaim one's history. Set in 1930s Pittsburgh, the play follows the story of the Charles family, who must confront the ghosts of their past as they grapple with the fate of a cherished family heirloom - an ornately carved piano that holds the key to their ancestral story. Tensions rise as the family is forced to choose between honoring their heritage or forging a new path forward.

Arthur Miller's Pulitzer Prize-winning masterpiece about Willy Loman, a traveling salesman grappling with the collapse of the American Dream. This 2022 revival at the Hudson Theatre, directed by Miranda Cromwell, stars Wendell Pierce and Sharon D Clarke.

"1776" brings the Founding Fathers to life in a captivating musical journey. As America teeters on the brink of revolution, a group of determined men, led by John Adams, fight passionately to declare independence and shape the future of a new nation. Filled with rousing songs and rich historical drama, this Tony-winning production offers a compelling look at the birth of the United States.

"Cost of Living" is a powerful exploration of the human experience, where the lives of four strangers intersect in unexpected ways. Marlee, a graduate student with cerebral palsy, and Eddie, a truck driver with a troubled past, forge an unlikely connection, while Jess, a wealthy woman, and John, her ex-husband, navigate the complexities of their relationship. As their stories unfold, the play delves into themes of disability, class, and the true cost of living, leaving audiences deeply moved and contemplating the depth of the human experience.

"Leopoldstadt" is a haunting exploration of a Jewish family's generational journey through the tumultuous 20th century. Spanning decades, the play delves into the personal lives and historical upheavals that reshape their identity and connection. Audiences are immersed in a poignant tale of love, loss, and the resilience of the human spirit as this family navigates the changing tides of a world in flux.

In the enchanting musical "Into the Woods," a baker and his wife embark on a magical journey, navigating the intertwining stories of classic fairy tale characters. As they traverse the mysterious woods, they encounter a cast of captivating characters, each with their own wishes and desires, leading to a thrilling exploration of the consequences of those wishes. With its blend of humor, heartbreak, and fairy tale wonder, "Into the Woods" weaves a spellbinding tale that delights audiences of all ages.

The Kite Runner is a stage adaption of Afghan-American novelist Khaled Hosseini's 2003 book The Kite Runner. Aside from sharing the 2003 book as a source, it is unrelated to the 2007 film The Kite Runner.
Playwright Conor McPherson reimagines the songs of the legendary Bob Dylan in GIRL FROM THE NORTH COUNTRY — the soaring masterwork about the dreams we keep close and the people we hold dear.

The Tragedy of Macbeth, often shortened to Macbeth (), is a tragedy by William Shakespeare, estimated to have been first performed in 1606. It dramatises the physically violent and damaging psychological effects of political ambitions and power.

Mr. Saturday Night tells the story of Buddy Young Jr., a once-famous comedian whose star has dimmed. As he grapples with the realities of an industry that has left him behind, Buddy must confront his complicated relationships with his family and his own legacy. With a score by the legendary composer and lyricist Billy Crystal, the musical offers a poignant and hilarious exploration of the triumphs and tragedies of a life in show business.

shows.t: POTUS: Or, Behind Every Great Dumbass are Seven Women Trying to Keep Him Alive
shows.syn: "POTUS: Or, Behind Every Great Dumbass are Seven Women Trying to Keep Him Alive" is a riotous comedy that takes audiences behind the closed doors of the Oval Office. As the President finds himself embroiled in a deepening scandal, a group of powerful women – from the First Lady to the White House secretary – must navigate the chaos and preserve the status quo, all while dealing with the Commander-in-Chief's increasingly erratic behavior. With its sharp wit and unapologetic take on politics, this Broadway show promises a rollicking good time.

Intimately explores the life of a queer Black man navigating self-doubt, societal pressures, and the cacophony of voices in his head. With raw honesty and biting humor, the story follows Usher's journey of self-discovery as he grapples with his identity and the complexities of being a Black, gay man in America. A profound, genre-defying musical that delves into the nuances of the human experience.

The Skin of Our Teeth is a play by Thornton Wilder that won the Pulitzer Prize for Drama. It opened on October 15, 1942, at the Shubert Theatre in New Haven, Connecticut, before moving to the Plymouth Theatre on Broadway on November 18, 1942.

Fanny Brice, a young Jewish woman with an extraordinary talent, dreams of becoming a star. As she rises to fame and success on the stage, her tumultuous relationship with her charming but unreliable husband, Nick Arnstein, takes center stage, leading to a bittersweet journey of love, ambition, and the relentless pursuit of the limelight.

In a small English town in 1965, the impending abolition of capital punishment sends shockwaves through the local community. As the town's former executioner grapples with his identity and purpose, a mysterious stranger arrives, sparking a tense and thrilling confrontation that unravels dark secrets and forces everyone to confront their own moral compass. Hangmen is a gripping, darkly comedic exploration of power, justice, and the consequences of our actions.

shows.t: for colored girls who have considered suicide / when the rainbow is enuf
shows.syn: "for colored girls who have considered suicide / when the rainbow is enuf" is a powerful and poetic theatrical experience that explores the joys, struggles, and resilience of Black women. Through a series of interconnected monologues, the play delves into themes of identity, love, and the strength that emerges from even the darkest of times. This acclaimed work by Ntozake Shange is a profound and moving celebration of the human spirit, inviting audiences to bear witness to the extraordinary stories of a diverse group of women.
How I Learned to Drive is a play written by American playwright Paula Vogel. The play premiered on March 16, 1997, Off-Broadway at the Vineyard Theatre.

shows.v: Harold and Miriam Steinberg Center for Theatre
shows.syn: The Minutes is a comedic play written by Tracy Letts. It had its premiere at the Steppenwolf Theater in Chicago on November 9, 2017, and began previews at the James Earl Jones Theatre in New York City on February 25, 2020.

In "American Buffalo," three men hatch a plan to steal a rare and valuable coin from a local collector. As they navigate the complexities of their scheme, tensions rise and loyalties are tested in this gritty and intense exploration of the American dream. With its powerful performances and gripping narrative, this Tony-nominated play offers a captivating glimpse into the underbelly of small-town America.
A dance, circus, and multimedia adaptation of Antoine de Saint-Exupery's classic tale.

Birthday Candles is a play by Noah Haidle. It was originally scheduled to open on April 2, 2020, but was delayed by the COVID-19 pandemic. It subsequently ran at the American Airlines Theatre from March 18 to May 29, 2022 (with opening night on April 10), starring Debra Messing as Ernestine Ashworth and directed by Messing's Tisch School of the Arts classmate Vivienne Benesch.

In the quirky supernatural comedy "Beetlejuice", a recently deceased young woman named Lydia discovers the mischievous and outrageous bio-exorcist Beetlejuice, who promises to help her connect with the other side. But when Beetlejuice's antics spiral out of control, Lydia and her family must find a way to stop him before he takes over the living world. With its wild visuals, infectious music, and a devilishly funny lead character, "Beetlejuice" is a wild, irreverent ride that will leave audiences thoroughly entertained.

Paradise Square is a stage musical, with music by Jason Howland, lyrics by Masi Asare and Nathan Tysen, and a book by Christina Anderson, Larry Kirwan and Craig Lucas. Set in New York City during the Civil War and the New York City draft riots, the musical follows conflict between Irish Americans and Black Americans.

Three interconnected stories unfold in the iconic Plaza Hotel suite, revealing the complexities of marriage, relationships, and the human condition. From a long-married couple confronting their past to a groom-to-be's encounter with his high school flame, "Plaza Suite" offers a hilarious and poignant exploration of the joys and challenges that arise within the confines of a single hotel room.

"The Music Man" follows the mischievous Harold Hill, a con man who arrives in a small Iowa town, promising to form a boys' marching band and sell them instruments and uniforms. As he begins to charm the townspeople, including the prim and proper Marian the Librarian, his scheme starts to unravel, leading to a heartwarming and hilarious conclusion that celebrates the power of music and community.
The story of Michael Jackson, centering around the making of his 1992 Dangerous World Tour, revealing the creative mind and collaborative spirit that catapulted him into legendary status.

In the heart of Detroit's auto industry, four factory workers face an uncertain future as the plant teeters on the brink of closure. As tensions rise and loyalties are tested, they must navigate the complexities of their relationships and the changing tides of economic upheaval. "Skeleton Crew" is a poignant and gripping exploration of the human cost of survival in the face of dwindling opportunities.

Flying Over Sunset is a musical with music by Tom Kitt, lyrics by Michael Korie, and a book by James Lapine. The musical is a fictional account of a meeting between Aldous Huxley, Clare Boothe Luce and Cary Grant, who all used the drug LSD.

"Company" follows the life of Robert, a single man navigating the complexities of relationships and marriage in New York City. On the eve of his 35th birthday, Robert's married friends attempt to set him up, leading him to confront his own commitment issues and the desire for a meaningful connection. With Stephen Sondheim's iconic score and a witty, insightful script, "Company" offers a poignant and humorous exploration of the joys and challenges of modern life.

Based on the beloved 1993 film, Mrs. Doubtfire follows Daniel Hillard, an out-of-work actor who disguises himself as a female housekeeper to spend time with his children after a bitter divorce. The musical features a book by Karey Kirkpatrick and John O'Brien.

Slave Play is a three-act play by Jeremy O. Harris  about race, sex, power relations, trauma, and interracial relationships. It follows three interracial couples undergoing "Antebellum Sexual Performance Therapy" because the black partners have begun struggling to feel arousal or pleasure when engaging sexually with their white partners.

"Clyde's" is a gritty, high-stakes drama that takes place in the kitchen of a truck stop diner. A motley crew of ex-convicts, led by the tough-as-nails Clyde, fights for a chance at redemption as they strive to perfect their culinary craft and escape the cycle of recidivism. With razor-sharp dialogue and a pulsing undercurrent of hope, the play explores the complexities of second chances and the power of community in the face of adversity.

In the groundbreaking Broadway production of "Trouble in Mind," a talented African American actress navigates the complex world of the theater, confronting racial prejudices and her own internal struggles. As she rehearses for a new play, she must confront the realities of a profession that often fails to recognize her humanity, leading to a powerful and thought-provoking exploration of the enduring legacy of racism in the arts. Audiences are captivated by this poignant and insightful drama that delves deep into the personal and professional lives of those fighting for their place on the stage.

"Diana, The Musical" transports audiences into the private life of the iconic Princess Diana as she navigates the unforgiving spotlight of the British royal family. Tracing her meteoric rise to global fame, the musical delves into the complexities of her marriage, her personal struggles, and her transformative impact on the world. With stunning visuals and a powerful score, this captivating production offers a unique glimpse into the life of a beloved figure who forever changed the course of history.

shows.v: Harold and Miriam Steinberg Center for Theatre
shows.syn: "Caroline, or Change" is a captivating musical that explores the complex relationship between a young Jewish boy and his family's African-American housekeeper in 1960s Louisiana. As tensions rise during a time of social upheaval, the two form an unexpected bond that challenges their preconceptions and illuminates the enduring power of human connection. With a powerful score by Tony Kushner and Jeanine Tesori, this deeply moving story invites audiences to witness the transformative journey of lives intertwined.

ct.n: Rick Bassett, Joseph Joubert and Buryl Red

shows.syn: Dana H. is a play written by Lucas Hnath, based on the real-life experiences of Hnath's mother Dana Higginbotham. It premiered on Broadway in 2021, winning two Tony Awards.

The Lehman Trilogy chronicles the remarkable rise and fall of the Lehman brothers, whose small dry goods store in Alabama evolves into a global financial powerhouse. Spanning over a century, this captivating drama follows three generations as they navigate the ever-changing landscape of American capitalism, ultimately leading to the collapse of their family's renowned investment firm. Audiences are immersed in an epic tale of ambition, greed, and the human cost of pursuing the American Dream.

Thoughts of a Colored Man is a play written by Keenan Scott II that opened on Broadway on October 13, 2021. It is Scott's Broadway debut, and the play is the first Broadway show that was written and directed by Black men (Steve Broadnax III) with a Black man in the lead role and lead produced by all Black artists.

ct.n: Toni-Leslie James and Devario D. Simmons

shows.syn: The Room is Harold Pinter's first play, written and first produced in 1957.

The Jenkins family gathers for their patriarch's funeral, but old tensions and new secrets threaten to tear them apart. As they navigate their differences, the family discovers the power of forgiveness and the importance of cherishing their connection, even in the face of adversity. "Chicken & Biscuits" is a heartwarming and hilarious exploration of family, legacy, and the enduring bonds that hold us together.

"Lackawanna Blues" transports audiences to 1950s Buffalo, where a young boy navigates a vibrant, eclectic community centered around the boarding house run by his loving, yet unconventional caregiver, Miss Rachel. Through a kaleidoscope of dynamic characters, this deeply personal story explores the transformative power of human connection and the resilience of the human spirit.
The six wives of Henry VIII take the mic to tell their own stories and reclaim their identities in this electrifying pop concert musical.

Jenna, a small-town waitress and expert pie maker, is trapped in an unhappy marriage. When an unexpected pregnancy and a pie-baking contest offer her a chance at a new life, Jenna must find the courage to confront her past and forge her own path to happiness. With an irresistible score and a poignant story of empowerment, "Waitress" takes audiences on a heartwarming journey of self-discovery.

ct.n: Sara Bareilles and The Waitress Band

shows.syn: In "Pass Over," two young Black men, Moses and Kitch, engage in a thought-provoking and often humorous dialogue as they navigate the harsh realities of life on the streets. Trapped in a cycle of violence and oppression, they dream of a promised land where they can finally find freedom and escape their bleak circumstances. As the play unfolds, it explores timely issues of race, inequality, and the struggle for justice, delivering a powerful and poignant theatrical experience.

Ivo van Hove's reimagined revival of the classic musical about rival New York City gangs, the Jets and the Sharks, and the ill-fated romance between Tony and Maria. The production featured bold multimedia staging and Anne Teresa De Keersmaeker's choreography.
A comedy about a couple in their 50s who shocks their adult sons by announcing they are getting divorced after nearly 50 years of marriage, upending the family's assumptions about love and happiness.

A Soldier's Play is a play by American playwright Charles Fuller. Set on a US Army installation in the segregation-era South, the play is a loose adaptation of Herman Melville's novella Billy Budd, and follows the murder investigation of the Sergeant in an all-black unit.

Award-winning actress Laura Linney returns to Broadway in the American premiere of a haunting new solo play adapted by Rona Munro from the bestselling novel by Pulitzer Prize winner Elizabeth Strout. A sold-out sensation originally produced by the London Theatre Company at the Bridge Theatre in London, Ms Linney was hailed as “luminous” by the The New York Times, “genuinely phenomenal” by Time Out London, and the play was called “deeply affecting and heartbreaking” by The Observer.

Joy, rage, love, heartache, strength, wisdom, catharsis, LIFE: everything we’ve been waiting and hoping to see on a Broadway stage for over a year is back, in this exhilarating, fearless new musical based on Alanis Morissette’s world-changing music.

A Christmas Carol is a play by Jack Thorne based on the 1843 novella of the same name by Charles Dickens.
The adaptation was written for The Old Vic in London where it premiered for the 2017 festive season and has returned every year since due to popular demand.

In contemporary Manhattan, Eric and Toby are 30-somethings who seem to be very much in love and thriving. But on the cusp of their engagement, they meet an older man haunted by the past, and a younger man hungry for a future. Chance meetings lead to surprising choices as the lives of three generations interlink and collide—with explosive results.

This new musical reveals a comeback story like no other, of a woman who dared to defy the bounds of racism, sexism and ageism to become the global Queen of Rock n’ Roll. TINA – THE TINA TURNER MUSICAL is a celebration of resilience and an inspiration of triumph over adversity.

shows.v: Harold and Miriam Steinberg Center for Theatre
shows.syn: Tony® and Emmy® Award winner Mary-Louise Parker returns to Broadway in the thrilling new show that’s been hailed as a New York Times Critics' Pick and “a gripping stunner of a 90-minute play” (Chicago Tribune). Written by Pulitzer Prize finalist Adam Rapp and directed by Tony Award winner David Cromer, "The Sound Inside" is truly riveting theatre about a brilliant Ivy League professor, a mysterious student and a troubling favor.

The Lightning Thief is a musical with music and lyrics by Rob Rokicki and a book by Joe Tracz, based on the 2005 novel of the same name by Rick Riordan. In the musical,  Percy Jackson - a teenage boy who discovers that he is a demigod - goes on a quest to find Zeus's missing lightning bolt to prevent a war between the Greek gods.

The Rose Tattoo  is a three-act play written by Tennessee Williams in 1949 and 1950; after its Chicago premiere on December 29, 1950, he made further revisions to the play for its Broadway premiere on February 2, 1951, and its publication by New Directions the following month. A film adaptation was released in 1955.

Buena Vista Social Club is a 2023 stage musical, with a book by Marco Ramirez and featuring the music recorded by the ensemble musician group Buena Vista Social Club.
The musical is set in Havana, Cuba and spans time from the 1950s to the 1990s, following the lives of four prominent musicians, the effect communism and the rise of Fidel Castro had on musicians at the time, and their eventual collaboration in 1997 on the landmark album Buena Vista Social Club.

Natasha, Pierre & The Great Comet of 1812 (or simply The Great Comet) is a sung-through musical adaptation of a 70-page segment from Leo Tolstoy's 1869 novel War and Peace. The show was written by composer, lyricist, playwright, orchestrator Dave Malloy and originally directed by Rachel Chavkin.

ct.n: Paul James Prendergast and Marc Salzberg

shows.syn: The Height of the Storm is a play by Florian Zeller which follows a couple looking back at 50 years of marriage and realising their relationship may not be as perfect as they expected.

Harold Pinter's searing exploration of an extramarital affair told in reverse chronology, from bitter aftermath to first illicit spark. This 2019 Jamie Lloyd-directed production stars Tom Hiddleston, Zawe Ashton, and Charlie Cox, transferring from a celebrated London run.

A double bill of two solo plays: "Sea Wall" by Simon Stephens (starring Tom Sturridge) and "A Life" by Nick Payne (starring Jake Gyllenhaal). Both monologues explore love, loss, and the moments that define us.
Enter a world of splendor and romance, of eye-popping excess, of glitz, grandeur and glory! A world where Bohemians and aristocrats rub elbows and revel in electrifying enchantment.

shows.t: Frankie and Johnny in the Clair de Lune
shows.syn: Frankie and Johnny in the Clair de Lune is a two-character play by Terrence McNally that was first performed off-Broadway in 1987.
Beetlejuice, Beetlejuice, Beetlejuice! Say it three times, and the musical adaptation of the 1988 comedy classic will appear for a third Broadway run!
Ink is a 2017 play by James Graham which premiered in London in 2017.

Tootsie is a musical comedy with music and lyrics by David Yazbek and a book by Robert Horn. The musical is based on the 1982 American comedy film of the same name written by Larry Gelbart and Barry Levinson.

Arthur Miller's devastating drama about a wartime manufacturing scandal that tears apart a middle-class family, exposing the moral costs of profit and complicity. This 2019 revival, directed by Jack O'Brien, stars Annette Bening and Tracy Letts.

shows.t: Gary: A Sequel to Titus Andronicus
shows.syn: Gary: A Sequel to Titus Andronicus is a play written by Taylor Mac. The play is set in the aftermath of William Shakespeare's Titus Andronicus. The play premiered on Broadway in April 2019 starring Nathan Lane and received seven Tony Award nominations.

Hillary and Clinton is a stage play written by Lucas Hnath that premiered in 2016 at the Victory Gardens Theater in Chicago. The play is set in an alternate universe and tells a story centering on Hillary Clinton's 2008 presidential campaign.

Welcome to Hadestown, where a song can change your fate. This acclaimed new musical by Anaïs Mitchell intertwines two mythic tales — that of Orpheus and Eurydice, and King Hades and his wife Persephone.
Burn This (stylized as Burn/This for the 2019 revival) is a play by Lanford Wilson. Like much of Wilson's work, the play includes themes of gay identity and relationships.
Oklahoma! is the first musical written by the duo of Rodgers and Hammerstein. The musical is based on Lynn Riggs's 1931 play, Green Grow the Lilacs.

The Tragedy of King Lear, often shortened to King Lear, is a tragedy written by William Shakespeare in late 1605 or early 1606. Set in pre-Roman Britain, the play depicts the consequences of King Lear's love-test, in which he divides his power and land according to the praise of his daughters.

shows.t: What the Constitution Means to Me
shows.syn: Fifteen-year-old Heidi Schreck put herself through college by giving speeches about the U.S. Constitution. Now, the Obie Award winner recalls her teenage self in order to trace the profound relationship between four generations of women in her own family and the founding document that dictated their rights and citizenship. This witty and searingly personal exploration breathes new life into our founding document and imagines how it will shape the next generation of American women.

Ain't Too Proud: The Life and Times of The Temptations is a 2017 jukebox musical with music and lyrics by The Temptations and a book by Dominique Morisseau. Based on the story of The Temptations, the musical had a series of regional productions and opened at Broadway's Imperial Theatre in March 2019.

ct.n: Steve Canyon Kennedy and Walter Trarbach

shows.v: Harold and Miriam Steinberg Center for Theatre
shows.syn: Kiss Me, Kate is a musical with music and lyrics by Cole Porter and a book by Bella and Samuel Spewack. The story involves the production of a musical version of William Shakespeare's The Taming of the Shrew and the conflict on and off-stage between Fred Graham, the show's director, producer, and star, and his leading lady, his ex-wife Lilli Vanessi.

ct.n: Michael Starobin and Ned Paul Ginsburg

shows.syn: Be More Chill is a musical with original music and lyrics by Joe Iconis, and a book by Joe Tracz, based on the 2004 novel of the same name by Ned Vizzini. After a 2015 regional theatre production, the musical premiered off-Broadway in 2018.

Opposites attack in Sam Shepard’s Pulitzer Prize-nominated play about two brothers with more in common than they think. Holed up in their mother’s California house, lowlife Lee (Ethan Hawke) and screenwriter Austin (Paul Dano) wrestle with big issues—and each other. Order vs. chaos. Art vs. commerce. Typewriter vs. toaster...Shepard’s rip-roaring classic returns to Broadway, gleefully detonating our misguided myths of family, identity and the American Dream.

Choir Boy is a coming-of-age play by American playwright Tarell Alvin McCraney. The play premiered in September 2012 at the Royal Court Theatre, London, before going on to play productions at New York City Center, Alliance Theatre, Geffen Playhouse, and many more regional theaters across the United States.

In Harper Lee's timeless tale, a young girl named Scout navigates the complexities of racial injustice in 1930s Alabama as her father, Atticus Finch, takes on a challenging case to defend a Black man wrongfully accused of a crime. Set against the backdrop of a sleepy Southern town, this poignant story explores themes of morality, courage, and the enduring power of compassion.
Network is a play by Lee Hall, adapted from the 1976 film of the same name which had an Academy Award–winning screenplay by Paddy Chayefsky and was directed by Sidney Lumet.

For six straight decades, only one unstoppable force has flat-out dominated popular culture — breaking down barriers, pushing boundaries, and letting nothing and no one stand in her way. "The Cher Show" is her story, and it’s packed with so much Cher that it takes three women to play her: the kid starting out, the glam pop star and the icon.

ct.n: Christine Jones and Brett J. Banakis

shows.syn: The Prom is a musical with music by Matthew Sklar, lyrics by Chad Beguelin, and a book by Bob Martin and Beguelin, based on an original concept by Jack Viertel. The musical follows four Broadway actors lamenting their days of fame, as they travel to the conservative town of Edgewater, Indiana, to help a lesbian student banned from bringing her girlfriend to high school prom.

Four Plays, or Moral Representations, in One is a Jacobean era stage play, one of the dramatic works in the canon of John Fletcher and his collaborators. Initially published in the first Beaumont and Fletcher folio of 1647, the play is notable both for its unusual form and for the question of its authorship.

KING KONG comes alive through a stunning mix of puppetry, music and stagecraft. This “epic thrill ride” (Variety) follows the ambitious actress Ann Darrow and the filmmaker who promises to make her a star. Together, they voyage from 1930s New York to an uncharted island to capture the world’s greatest wonder. At the center of this theatrical reimagining: a 20-foot high, 2,000-pound ape and the woman who risks everything to help him.

A Florida police station in the middle of the night; a mother searching for her missing teenage son. "American Son" is a gripping tale of two parents caught in our national divide, with their worst fears hanging in the balance.

After a smash-hit run Off-Broadway, HARVEY FIERSTEIN's "Torch Song" heads to Broadway for a strictly limited engagement. This critically acclaimed production of the Tony Award-winning comedy stars Drama Desk Award winner MICHAEL URIE ("Younger," "Ugly Betty," "Buyer & Cellar") and Academy Award and Tony winner MERCEDES RUEHL ("The Fisher King," "Lost in Yonkers") and is directed by Tony nominee MOISÉS KAUFMAN ("The Laramie Project," "I Am My Own Wife").
Iconic actor, comedian, writer, and director Elaine May returns to Broadway for the first time in 50 years in Academy Award winner Kenneth Lonergan’s acclaimed memory play, "The Waverly Gallery."

The Carney farmhouse is a hive of activity with preparations for the annual harvest. A day of hard work on the land and a traditional night of feasting and celebrations lie ahead. But this year they will be interrupted by a visitor.

shows.v: Harold and Miriam Steinberg Center for Theatre
shows.syn: The determined young fact checker (Daniel Radcliffe) is about to stir up trouble. The demanding editor (Cherry Jones) has given him a big assignment: apply his skill to a groundbreaking piece by the unorthodox author (Bobby Cannavale).

Bianca Leigh is an American actress. She appeared as Mary Ellen in the 2005 film Transamerica, and Karma Johnstone in the 2016 film Hurricane Bianca and the sequel Hurricane Bianca: From Russia with Hate.
The Tragedy of Hamlet, Prince of Denmark, often shortened to Hamlet (), is a tragedy written by William Shakespeare sometime between 1599 and 1601. It is Shakespeare's longest play.
Pretty Woman is a 1990 American romantic comedy-drama film directed by Garry Marshall and written by J. F. Lawton.

ct.n: Kenneth Posner and Philip S. Rosenberg

shows.syn: Gettin' the Band Back Together is a musical with music and lyrics by Mark Allen and a book by Ken Davenport and the Grundleshotz, a group of performers and writers including Sebastian Arcelus, Fred Berman, Michael Hirstreet, Jenna Coker Jones, Craig Jorczak, Nathan Kaufman, Jay Klaitz, Emily McNamara, Jennifer Miller, Bhavesh Patel, Sarah Saltzberg and Fletcher Young.

Head Over Heels is a jukebox musical comedy with music and lyrics from the catalog of The Go-Go's. Jeff Whitty conceived the show and wrote the original book, which was then adapted by James Magruder after Whitty left the show due to conflict with incoming director Michael Mayer.

It’s Christmas Eve, and Ed has gathered his three adult sons to celebrate with matching pajamas, trash-talking, and Chinese takeout. But when a question they can’t answer interrupts their holiday cheer, they are forced to confront their own identities. Obie Award-winning playwright Young Jean Lee takes a hilariously ruthless look at the classic American father-son drama. This is one white Christmas like you’ve never seen before.

Celebrating its 50th Anniversary, the groundbreaking comic drama THE BOYS IN THE BAND will appear on Broadway for the first time ever in a strictly limited 15-week engagement this spring. MART CROWLEY’s landmark 1968 play centers on a group of gay men who gather in a NYC apartment for a friend’s birthday party. After the drinks are poured and the music turned up, the evening slowly exposes the fault-lines beneath their friendships and the self-inflicted heartache that threatens their solidarity.
Two-time Oscar winner and Tony Award winner DENZEL WASHINGTON returns to Broadway in one of the signal roles in the American theatre in Eugene O’Neill’s THE ICEMAN COMETH, for 14 weeks only.
Saint Joan is a play by George Bernard Shaw about the 15th-century French military figure Joan of Arc. It is divided into six scenes and a final epilogue.

Roundabout Theatre Company’s dazzling new production of "Travesties", Tom Stoppard’s 1976 Tony Award-winning Best Play, has arrived—and The New York Times says it’s “ridiculously entertaining.” An artist, a writer and a revolutionary collide in this “giddy, head-spinning triumph” (Entertainment Weekly), a kaleidoscopic thrill-ride through the worlds of art and revolution in 1917 Switzerland…and in the maze of one man’s memory.

She was a girl from Boston with a voice from heaven who shot through the stars from gospel choir to dance floor diva. But what the world didn’t know was how Donna Summer risked it all to break through barriers, becoming the icon of an era and the supreme queen for every diva who followed.

shows.t: Harry Potter and the Cursed Child
shows.syn: Nineteen years after Harry's original adventure, he's now a Ministry of Magic employee while his son Albus struggles with the family legacy at Hogwarts.

Don’t miss Lerner & Loewe’s MY FAIR LADY at Lincoln Center Theater, now playing through July 7 only! Directed by Tony® winner Bartlett Sher, this “enchanting and incandescent” (The Washington Post) revival was named the best Broadway musical of 2018 by The New York Times, Entertainment Weekly and Time Magazine. NY1 raves, “it reminds you how indispensable great theater can be.” The New York Times calls it “thrilling, glorious and better than it ever was.”

Rodgers & Hammerstein’s "Carousel" has returned to Broadway in a sumptuous production directed by three-time Tony winner Jack O’Brien, starring Joshua Henry and Jessie Mueller, with choreography by New York City Ballet’s Justin Peck. “God is in CAROUSEL,” raves The Chicago Tribune. “It’s the greatest musical ever written.”  Don’t miss the ecstatically acclaimed revival NPR calls “the best musical of the season, by far.”

shows.v: Harold and Miriam Steinberg Center for Theatre
shows.syn: “If you can see one play on Broadway, it has to be CHILDREN OF A LESSER GOD” declares United Press International. The New York Times calls it “a play unlike any other” and New York Post says it’s “a joyous event full of love and passion. You will never forget it.”

===Act One=== Janis Sarkisian and Damian Hubbard introduce the audience to Cady Heron, breaking the fourth wall and inviting them on their journey ("A Cautionary Tale"). Cady and her parents have moved to a Chicago suburb from Kenya, but Cady soon realizes that her high school classmates do not readily accept others who differ from them.

Two-time Academy Award winner GLENDA JACKSON makes her long-awaited return to Broadway alongside three-time Emmy and Tony winner LAURIE METCALF and Tony nominee ALISON PILL in the Broadway premiere of Edward Albee’s Pulitzer Prize-winning masterpiece, "Three Tall Women."
Lobby Hero is a play by Kenneth Lonergan. It premiered off-Broadway in 2001.
A quarter-century after stunning the theatre world, one of the greatest theatrical journeys of our time returns to Broadway in an acclaimed new production from the National Theatre.
For the first time, "Frozen" comes to life on Broadway, re-imagined for the stage in a new production by an award-winning team.
Welcome to Margaritaville, where people come to get away from it all—and stay to find something they never expected.

Oscar and three-time Tony winner Mark Rylance, following his acclaimed performance in the hit film Dunkirk, returns to the stage in "Farinelli and the King". This captivating new play arrives on Broadway after sold-out runs at Shakespeare’s Globe and on London’s West End.
The Children is a play written by Lucy Kirkwood which premiered in London in 2016 and then on Broadway in 2017.
A legendary roster of Grammy Award winners. A visionary director and Tony Award-winning design team. One of the world’s most beloved characters. Turn them loose on Broadway and what do you get?

production. ]] One stormy night in the Antilles archipelago, thunder booms, making a small girl cry in fear. To comfort her, the village storytellers tell her the story of Ti Moune, a peasant girl who falls in love with a grand homme, Daniel Beauxhomme – a story of life, pain, love, grief, faith, and hope.

|setting=Late Fall, Washington, D. |place =South Coast Repertory, Costa Mesa, California |genre=Drama |writer=Beau Willimon |caption=}} The Parisian Woman is a 2013 play by Beau Willimon. It premiered at the South Coast Repertory in April 2013 and centers on Chloe, a socialite armed with charm and wit, coming to terms with politics, her past, her marriage and an uncertain future.
Meteor Shower is a 2016 play written by Steve Martin. The play, a comedy, is set in 1993 in Ojai, California. It premiered on Broadway in 2017.

shows.v: Harold and Miriam Steinberg Center for Theatre
shows.syn: A sold-out run at The Public Theater. A record-breaking engagement at Berkeley Rep. Endless critical and audience acclaim. Chalk it all up to the mad genius of Emmy Award winner John Leguizamo, standing at the head of the class in his one-man tour de force, LATIN HISTORY FOR MORONS.

THE BAND’S VISIT is the winner of 10 Tony Awards, making it one of the most Tony-winning musicals in history. In this delightfully offbeat story, set in a town that’s way off the beaten path, a band of musicians arrive lost, out of the blue. Under the spell of the desert sky, and with beautiful music perfuming the air, the band brings the town to life in unexpected and tantalizing ways. Even the briefest visit can stay with you forever.
Junk: The Golden Age of Debt is a 2016 play by Ayad Akhtar. It premiered at the La Jolla Playhouse in July 2016 and then on Broadway in 2017 and centers on a corporate takeover.

M. Butterfly is a play by David Henry Hwang. The story, while entwined with that of the opera Madama Butterfly, is based most directly on the relationship between French diplomat Bernard Boursicot and Shi Pei Pu, a Beijing opera singer.

Time and the Conways is in three acts. The first act is set in the Conway house in 1919 on the night of the birthday of one of the daughters, Kay. Act Two moves to the same night in 1937 and is set in the same room in the house. Act Three then returns to 1919, seconds after Act One left off. In the first Act we meet the Conway family, Mrs Conway, her daughters Kay, Hazel, Madge and Carol and her sons Alan and Robin.
Prince of Broadway is a musical revue showcasing the producing career of Harold Prince. Prince himself directed the production, his final Broadway credit.

Michael Francis Moore (born April 23, 1954) is an American film director, producer, screenwriter, and author. Moore's work frequently addresses various social, political, and economic topics. He first became publicly known for his award-winning debut documentary Roger & Me, a scathing look at the downfall of the automotive industry in 1980s Flint and Detroit.
Marvin's Room is a play by the American writer Scott McPherson. It tells the story of Bessie and her estranged sister, Lee, who confront a family crisis.
1984 is a 2013 play by Robert Icke and Duncan Macmillan based on the 1949 novel Nineteen Eighty-Four by George Orwell.
A Doll's House, Part 2 is a 2017 play written by Lucas Hnath. The play premiered at the South Coast Repertory, in April 2017, before transferring to Broadway at the John Golden Theatre.

===Act I=== Newly back from the front lines, the young vet, pianist, and singer/songwriter Donny Novitski returns home from the war to Cleveland, Ohio in 1945, to find an America eager to get back to life ("Just Like It Was Before"). Each of the members of the band have struggled with the adjustment from military life to life at home, but they find friendship and commonality over this shared goal.

A young black man named Paul shows up at the home of art dealer Flan Kittredge and his wife Louisa, known simply as "Ouisa", who live overlooking Central Park in New York City. Paul has a minor stab wound from an attempted mugging, and says he's a friend of their children at Harvard University. The Kittredges are trying to get the money to buy a painting by Paul Cézanne and now have this wounded stranger in their home.

From the Tony Award-winning creators of the Broadway classic Ragtime, this dazzling show transports us from the twilight of the Russian Empire to the euphoria of Paris in the 1920s, as a brave young woman sets out to discover the mystery of her past. Pursued by a ruthless Soviet officer determined to silence her, Anya enlists the aid of a dashing conman and a lovable ex-aristocrat. Together, they embark on an epic adventure to help her find home, love, and family.

===London=== ; Act I "Creation Overture" ††† - Willy Wonka "Almost Nearly Perfect" - Charlie Bucket "The Amazing Fantastical History of Mr. Willy Wonka" - Grandparents "A Letter from Charlie Bucket" - Charlie Bucket, Mr. Bucket, Mrs. Bucket, and Grandparents "More of Him to Love" - Mrs. Gloop, Augustus Gloop, and Mr.

Winner of 4 Tony Awards including Best Revival of a Musical, this universally acclaimed new production of "Hello, Dolly!' “is making people crazy happy” (The Washington Post).
