Cold-case investigation unfolds through a hybrid of dance, theatre, and fractured memory. Set within an unresolved crime reopened after twelve years, Scorched Earth follows a detective re-examining an old case rooted in questions of land, legacy, and personal conflict. As the investigation revisits conflicting accounts, the detective and the primary suspect move through shifting layers of recollection, imagination, and unresolved tension. Inspired by John B. Keane’s The Field, the production blends elements of crime drama and psychological inquiry with movement-based storytelling. The creative team includes director, choreographer, and performer Luke Murphy, with design by Alyson Cummins, lighting by Stephen Dodd, and music by Rob Moloney, reuniting collaborators from the multi-award-wi...

shows.t: Milo Rau & Édouard Louis: The Interrogation

shows.syn: ===Act 1=== Charlie Price grows up as the fourth-generation scion in the family business, Price & Son, a shoe manufacturing company with its factory in Northampton. Another young boy, growing up in London, is as fascinated by shoes as Charlie is bored by them. But in this case, it is a pair of red women's heels that have attracted his attention, aggravating his strict father.

Benjamin Millepied’s dance-drama uses live cameras and rotating casts in a site-wide staging. This production utilizes handheld cameras to expand the scope of the performance beyond the limits of a conventional stage. Live video footage is transmitted to screens onstage, tracking the action as it unfolds in various tableaux and locations both backstage and throughout the Armory venue. The dance-drama explores the intersection between theatrical artifice and reality. It features a concise adaptation of Shakespeare’s narrative, focusing solely on the core elements: the undying devotion of the central couple and the intense emotional fallout. A distinct element of the production is the use of a rotating cast for the star-crossed lovers in each performance. This approach deliberately deviat...

Five Texas teens raise calves and navigate faith, girlhood, and competition in a rural barn. In a rural East Texas barn, five teenage girls prepare calves for competition while navigating the tangled terrain of adolescence. As they wrestle with school expectations, religious beliefs, and the complex rules of small-town life, their bonds are tested in unexpected ways. Faith, friendship, and survival collide in a world where tenderness can be as unruly as the animals they raise. Written by Libby Carr, Calf Scramble presents a darkly comedic take on the pressures of growing up under the gaze of both peers and tradition. The piece captures the distinctive rhythms of rural life, framing the girls’ struggles against a backdrop of competition and scripture. Produced by Primary Stages, the work...

shows.t: How My Grandparents Fell in Love, A Musical
shows.syn: A musical set in 1930s Rovno where a returning immigrant pursues a scholar amid rising antisemitism. Set in 1933, this musical follows Charlie, a Polish-born Jew who returns to Rovno after a decade in America to find a bride, and Chava, a young woman working in a hat shop who hopes to continue her studies. As Charlie attempts to court her, Chava remains focused on her academic ambitions while political tensions and antisemitism grow across Europe. The work is created by the team behind The Sabbath Girl: A New Musical and is based on a true story.

shows.v: The Marjorie S. Deane Little Theater
shows.syn: A musical revue of new songs about ageing, change, and life from Maltby and Shire. This musical revue navigates themes of ageing, memory, love, family, and the everyday quirks of life’s later chapters through an evening of interconnected songs. Drawing on decades of collaboration, the work presents vignettes about long-ago romance, ambitions past and present, relationships with grandchildren, lost keys, and the ongoing rhythm of human experience. Presented Off-Broadway at the Marjorie S. Deane Little Theater from late February through early April 2026, the revue completes a loose trilogy of song collections by Maltby and Shire that began with their earlier anthologies Starting Here, Starting Now and Closer Than Ever.

shows.t: La Breve y Maravillosa Vida de Oscar Wao
shows.syn: The first-ever stage adaptation in Spanish of this renowned masterpiece.

Grimsby wants Eric to return to court to fulfill his birthright as king. However, Eric hears a beautiful voice and commands it to be followed .

shows.v: New York City Center - Stage II
shows.syn: LIL is a fighter, but not as well-known as her older brother BIG. What happens when she shows up on his doorstep and challenges him to a match?
The Dinosaurs is a piercing and funny look at female friendships, and the innately human battle between holding on and letting go.

shows.syn: *BIGFOOT! The Musical * is a musical myth about the small-town corruptions and paranoia that swirl around a misunderstood youth...who happens to be eight feet tall.

shows.syn: A two-hander comedy about heartbreak blending live music, science, and storytelling. The show follows Karin McCracken as a woman navigating the fallout of heartbreak, tracing a series of relationships, bad dates, and attempted reinventions. Opposite her, Simon Leary performs all the men in her life, shifting roles to reflect the recurring patterns and emotional echoes that define her experience. The narrative moves between humor and vulnerability, punctuated by lo-fi covers of well-known breakup songs that underscore the emotional terrain. Created by New Zealand company EBKM, the production integrates elements of science and technology to examine how heartbreak affects both body and mind. Through performance and music, the piece constructs a portrait of grief that situates romantic loss...

To booze or not to booze, that is the question at this hidden gem of the New York theatre scene. Even tragedies become comedies when four sober actors and one drunk one attempt to perform a Shakespeare play. The audience can even drink along.
Performed by the playwright and his mother, *Rheology* is a an expansive rumination on death, physics, and mother-son love.
*Jerome* is a new play about an aging gay couple who meets a stranger in their Arizona ghost town.

shows.v: Atlantic Theater Company - Linda Gross Theater
shows.syn: *Indian Princesses* is a touching and wise new play about a summer camp for young girls of color and their white fathers.

shows.t: El Último Sueño de Frida y Diego
shows.syn: Frida Kahlo and Diego Rivera reunite on Día de los Muertos in this vivid, magical-realist opera. Gabriela Lena Frank’s first opera brings Frida Kahlo and Diego Rivera’s love story to life in a stunning magical-realist tale. On Día de los Muertos, Frida returns from the underworld to reunite briefly with Diego, reliving their fiery passion and pain before parting once more. With a libretto by Pulitzer Prize winner Nilo Cruz, this visually striking production, inspired by their artwork, features Isabel Leonard and Carlos Álvarez under the baton of Yannick Nézet-Séguin. The Met premiere of Frank’s opera has been praised for its rich, imaginative score and vibrant storytelling.
*You Got Older* is a disarming play about a wayward daughter, her ailing father, and the strange connections she finds after returning home.
*Animal Wisdom* is a a musical séance like no other, a singular piece from Heather Christian blending storytelling, requiem, and family mythology.
*Burnout Paradise* is a unique theatrical experience in which four performers race on treadmills to get tasks done.

shows.v: The Newman Mills Theatre at the Robert W. Wilson MCC Theatre Space
shows.syn: *Birthright* is a new play about six friends, faith, and what it means to belong.
*My Joy is Heavy* is a musical memoir following a young family yearning for connection in the aftermath of a miscarriage.
*||: Girls :||: Chance :||: Music :||* is about a prestigious girls’ music program in Berkeley where four teens meet and grow together.

shows.v: Lincoln Center Theater - Mitzi E. Newhouse Theater
shows.syn: *The Whoopi Monologues* brings back characters, stories, and monologues by the acclaimed comedian in this special theatrical experience.
*The Emporium* is Thornton Wilder's final play, tracing a man who goes on a life-changing journey.
*Bughouse* is a singular new play about the life of Henry Darger, a recluse and artist of unparalleled potential.

shows.t: Giulia: The Poison Queen of Palermo
shows.syn: A true story of deadly secrets, justice, and a woman on the edge of becoming a legend. A single act of self-defense sets off a chain of events that turns Giulia Tofana into one of history’s most notorious serial killers. As she fights to free women from the monsters around them, she must confront a haunting question—can she seek justice without losing herself in the process? This world premiere musical unravels a gripping true story, woven with dangerous secrets and life-altering consequences, all set to a powerful, contemporary score.

shows.syn: The West End transfer of Anaïs Mitchell's Tony Award-winning musical, a retelling of the ancient Greek myth of Orpheus and Eurydice set in a post-apocalyptic world.
Cole Escola's wickedly funny reimagining of Mary Todd Lincoln as a woman desperate to become a stand-up comedian while her husband deals with some pesky Civil War business.
HAMILTON is an award-winning West End musical production about the life of Alexander Hamilton, the American Founding Father.
Now stumbling through its fourth catastrophic year, The Play That Goes Wrong is guaranteed to leave you aching with laughter!

shows.t: I Wish My Life Were Like A Musical
shows.syn: A comedic musical revealing the hilarious highs and lows of life as a theatre performer. I Wish My Life Were Like A Musical is a cheeky, song-filled look behind the curtain of musical theatre, charting the journey from eager drama school hopeful to seasoned diva, chorus veteran, or forgotten performer. With sharp humor and affectionate honesty, the show exposes everything performers never admit—from disastrous auditions and grueling choreography to backstage rivalries, fan encounters, and the rare onstage moments that make it all worthwhile. After earning numerous awards, multiple international productions, and even a filmed version for streaming, this hit revue brings its trademark mix of charm, chaos, and industry insider wit to New York’s SoHo Playhouse in 2026.
